March 30, 1983 
Elaine A. Hale, Director 
Santa Maria Public Airport District 
c/o 318 South Scott Drive 
Santa Maria, CA 
93454 
Dea r Ms. Hal e : 
Re: 
Your Request for Written 
Advice, Our File No. A-83-040 
You have requested our aaVlce regarding your duties and 
obligations under the recently enacted provisions of Government 
Code Section 84308.1/ 
The facts of your situation are as 
follows: 
Facts 
1. 
In 1980, you were elected to the position of Director of 
the Santa Maria Public Airport District ("District"). 
Your 
current term of four years expires in 1984 and you anticipate 
seeking re-election at that time. 
2. 
During 1982, you engaged in considerable fund-raising 
activities on behalf of a political action committee (PAC) and 
on behalf of a candidate for statewide office. 
On at least one 
occasion ten couples gave $250 at your request to a committee of 
Carol Hallett. 
3. 
During 1983, you contemplate working for Republican 
Assembly Leader Robert Naylor to raise campaign funds for 
AR-PACi this would be a paid position. 
4. 
Potential contributors whom you might solicit for 
contributions to AR-PAC include some who have planes at the 
Santa Maria Public Airport or have businesses located at the 
airport. 
1/ All statutory references are to the Government Code 
unless otherwise noted. 


Ms. Elaine A. Hale 
Ma rch 30, 1983 
Page 2 
5. 
You also anticipate a possible appointment at some 
future time to the Local Agency Formation Commission ("LAFCO") 
as the District's representative on that body. 
However, no such 
an appointment is likely to be made this year. 
ANALYSIS 
As I advised in my previous letter to you, your position as 
a director of the Santa Maria Airport District does not trigger 
the provisions of section 84308. 
Since the district directors 
are directly elected by the voters, the district is exempted 
from the coverage of this section. 
Section 84308 (d)i 2 Cal. 
Adm. Code Section 18438.1. 
If, at some future date, you are appointed to the LAFCO, 
Section 84308 will apply to you. 
The LAFCO is not directly 
elected by the voters, and thus is not exempt from Section 84308. 
As a member of the LAFCO, you would be subject to the 
prohibitions and the disclosure and disqualification provisions 
of Section 84308. 
This section would apply whenever you 
received, solicited, or directed£/ campaign contributions for 
your own campaign or for AR-PAC, from any applicant or his or 
her agent,l/ or any person who supports or opposes an 
application,!/ or his or her agent, in any proceeding 
involving a license, permit, or other entitlement for use.2/ 
If you are in fact appointed to the LAFCO, and wish to know 
whether any specific proceeding is covered by Section 84308, 
whether any specific contribution is prohibited, or whether 
disqualification is required in any specific instance, you 
should contact us again for more specific advice. 
I hope this letter helps answer your questions. 
I enclose a 
copy of our regulations for your review.

February 7, 1983 
Mr. Robert Leidigh 
Clarine 7l Xle 
3/8 
r:J)rive 
JJfaria. C7I 93454 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
Dear Mr. Leidigh: 
As per your request during our phone conversation of February 1, 
I will attempt to clarify my particular political situation as it 
pertains to fund raising. 
In the past I have worked as an advance person for Western Growers 
Association. 
This was my only paid position while I held an 
elected office. 
I was employed by WGA for four months of 1982 
(July - October). 
Their political action committee paid me to 
organize and maintain assembly of their planned fund raisers. 
During 1982 I also was involved in soliciting donations of up to 
$100 per person for various candidates and issues. 
To my best 
knowledge, no one I contacted gave more than that requested amount, 
except a host committee for a Hallett fund raiser. 
Each one of 
ten couples gave $250 "seed money" as hosts to get us off the ground 
on expenses. 
This went directly to Hallett's campaign committee 
in Sacramento. 
I did organize the event and requested these dona-
tions from those ten couples. 
All of the other admissions were 
$25 per person. 
I will not be involved in non-paying political fund raising during 
1983. 
At the present time I am contemplating a contract with 
AR-PAC under Assemblyman Robert Naylor. 
I would receive a percen-
tage per event. 
This would be a state-wide program. 
I would be 
soliciting in the Santa Maria area. 
A few potential donors have 
planes on the airport at which I hold my elected position, two 
have businesses on the airport at the present time. 
I have been elected to the position of Airport Director for a four-
year term. 
I will, in all probability, run again in 1984. 
I may 
be appointed to LAFCO within the next six weeks. 
It is my under-
standing that by law all special districts are allowed one appoint-
ment to LAFCO. 
I am primarily interested in avoiding any further public issue 
about my fund raising activities and would appreciate a letter 


Mr. Robert Leidigh 
February 7, 1983 
Page 2 
from your office delineating what is and is not legal for my 
personal situation. 
Thank you for your assistance in this matter. 
bw 
Elaine A. Hale 
Director 
Santa Maria Public Airport District

sections.question: What are the duties and obligations under Government Code Section 84308.1 for an elected Airport District Director who is engaged in fund-raising activities for political action committees and candidates, and who may be appointed to the Local Agency Formation Commission (LAFCO)?
sections.conclusion: Your position as a director of the Santa Maria Airport District does not trigger the provisions of section 84308, as district directors are directly elected by voters and are exempt from this section. However, if you are appointed to LAFCO, Section 84308 will apply to you, and you would be subject to prohibitions on soliciting or directing campaign contributions from applicants, supporters/opponents, or their agents in any proceedings involving licenses, permits, or entitlements.

sections.question_synthetic: Does Government Code Section 84308 apply to an Airport District Director's fund-raising activities, and what restrictions would apply if she is appointed to LAFCO?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Section 84308 does not apply to the requester's current position as an elected Airport District Director, but would apply if she is appointed to LAFCO, at which point she would face restrictions on soliciting campaign contributions related to LAFCO proceedings. She should contact the FPPC again if appointed to LAFCO for guidance on specific situations.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document includes both the original incoming request letter (February 7, 1983) and the FPPC response letter (March 30, 1983). Response clearly identifies facts, analysis, and conclusion sections. Extracted from the FPPC response letter as the authoritative source.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the duties and obligations under Government Code Section 84308.1 for an elected Airport District Director who is engaged in fund-raising activities for political action committees and candidates, and who may be appointed to the Local Agency Formation Commission (LAFCO)?

CONCLUSION: Your position as a director of the Santa Maria Airport District does not trigger the provisions of section 84308, as district directors are directly elected by voters and are exempt from this section. However, if you are appointed to LAFCO, Section 84308 will apply to you, and you would be subject to prohibitions on soliciting or directing campaign contributions from applicants, supporters/opponents, or their agents in any proceedings involving licenses, permits, or entitlements.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to an Airport District Director's inquiry about compliance with Government Code Section 84308 regarding her fund-raising activities. The advice clarifies that while her current elected position is exempt, if she is appointed to LAFCO, she would face restrictions on soliciting campaign contributions from parties involved in LAFCO proceedings.
